# Wave 6-15: 100 App Ideas
**The Spin-Off** — A motorized chair that rotates you away from the audience faster the more off-key you sing, literally turning your back on the crowd with every missed note.

**Spectral Echo** — Motion sensors detect your movements, then trigger spooky sounds and LED flashes 15 seconds later at that exact spot, creating an eerie ghost that retraces your footsteps.

**The Argument Historian** — AI that instantly reframes your couple's fight as a famous historical conflict (Cold War, civil dispute, trade war) with auto-generated peace treaties based on actual conflict resolutions.

**The Meteorological Regret Machine** — Every weather complaint button press prints a thermal receipt with an AI-generated apology from the sky itself, getting exponentially more desperate and pathetic with each complaint.

**Bad Password Buzzout** — Type your real password on a hidden keypad; weak ones trigger escalating buzzer sounds while a servo-controlled finger wags in shame and an LED strip flashes red.

**Pitch Roulette** — Spin to get a random absurd startup idea, deliver your 60-second pitch to the camera while the device mocks your confidence level and facial expressions with sarcastic VC commentary.

**This Day's Slots** — Pull a lever to spin three motorized reels landing on random years from history, then a thermal printer ejects a "winning ticket" revealing what happened on March 29 of each year.

**The Emoji Heist** — Multiplayer cooperative game where you plan and execute a virtual heist entirely through emoji communication with teammates and an AI security system that responds only in emoji.

**The Office Confessional** — Pressure sensor detects when you sit at your desk, a camera watches your work habits, and a speaker broadcasts dramatic reality TV narration of your productivity with music stings, shocking statistics about your coffee breaks, and running commentary on your typing intensity.

**Trash Talk Trash Can** — A motion-sensing trash bin that plays victory fanfare for successful throws and sad trombone for misses, with real-time accuracy tracking on a mini display.
